note: Long-run cultural/institutional-transmission skeptical lens. Built 2026-06-24 to sit OPPOSITE T_immigrationist_steelman (benefit) and COMPLEMENT S_restrictionist_steelman (short/medium-run fiscal cost). Lettered V (not U — U_source_incentive_meta already exists in lifetime_generators; the ledger-map's 'A-T' was stale). The steelmanned thesis: first-generation fiscal and crime ledgers — including everything this repo has measured — cannot see the deepest skeptical channel, namely that immigrants and (especially) their descendants partially transmit the culture, trust, institutions, and human-capital norms of their origin, which are economically consequential and persistent across generations. IF transmission is strong and slow-decaying AND origins are low-trust/low-institutional-quality, THEN large inflows could erode host institutional quality over decades — a cost no first-gen number captures. This cluster carries its own disconfirmation by construction: V08 is an explicit assimilation NULL, and V06 (national cognitive capital) is flagged as the contested, data-poor lens to STRESS-TEST, never to assume. Sources are REAL corpus entries (corpus:// URIs to fetched full text), not placeholder paths — the lesson from cluster-T. [FRAMING-SENSITIVE] throughout. The national-IQ / deep-roots data-quality fights live canonically in the sister repo ~/Projects/iq-sex-differences; this cluster cross-references them rather than re-litigating.

generators.name: Deep-roots / ancestry-adjusted development
generators.prompt: Before treating an origin group's economic outcomes as set by current host institutions, ask what the deep-roots literature predicts: long-run development correlates with the ANCESTRY-weighted history of a population (early state experience, agricultural transition, technology adoption) more than with the geography it currently occupies. For any migration claim, separate traits that travel WITH people across borders from traits that stay with PLACES.
generators.retrodiction: Would have flagged that cross-country development gaps shrink but do not vanish when you ancestry-adjust via the Putterman-Weil post-1500 migration matrix — i.e., a measurable component of what looks like 'bad geography' actually travels with populations. That component is the long-run channel a first-generation fiscal or crime ledger is structurally blind to.
generators.negative_space: Fiscal and crime ledgers are proximate and first-generation; the deep, ancestry-linked component of long-run development sits entirely outside their measurement window.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Origin-linked traits reset to the host mean on arrival
Long-run development is a property of places, not of the populations that inhabit them
The first generation is representative of the dynasty's long-run contribution
source_rel_paths: Putterman-Weil 2010 QJE 'Post-1500 Population Flows' (S2 CorpusId 5109434; ancestry-adjustment matrix) — identified, fetch pending
generators.theory_tested: Ancestry-adjusted origin-development indices should retain predictive power for 2nd-generation US outcomes beyond first-gen education, IF an ancestry index is merged onto the IPUMS panel by parental BPL. Bounded by V08 (regression toward host mean).

generators.name: Cultural transmission / epidemiological approach
generators.prompt: Identify cultural traits (work norms, female labor supply, fertility, saving) by the EPIDEMIOLOGICAL method: compare 2nd-generation immigrants from different origins living under the SAME US institutions. Origin differences that persist under fixed institutions are transmitted CULTURE, not contemporaneous environment — and they are an input the fiscal ledger treats as host-determined.
generators.retrodiction: Would have shown that 2nd-generation female labor supply and fertility still track the origin country's values (Fernandez-Fogli) even holding US institutions constant — culture persists at least one generation, so 'the children are just Americans' is an overstatement for the traits that matter to labor supply and dependency.
generators.negative_space: Cost and benefit ledgers alike attribute 2nd-gen behavior to host institutions; the transmitted-culture component, identified by the epidemiological design, is uncounted.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: 2nd-generation behavior is set by host institutions alone
Culture is fully assimilated by birth in the host country
Female labor supply and fertility are origin-invariant by the 2nd generation
source_rel_paths: Fernandez-Fogli 'Culture: An Empirical Investigation of Beliefs, Work, and Fertility' AEJ:Macro 2009 (DOI 10.1257/mac.1.1.146; S2 CorpusId 15277229) — saved, fetch pending
generators.theory_tested: Within-US 2nd-generation outcomes by origin (IPUMS, parental BPL) should retain an origin-culture component (female LFP, fertility) after controlling for education and current location.

generators.name: Inherited trust as a slow social-capital channel
generators.prompt: For outcomes that depend on social capital (contract enforcement, public-goods provision, low corruption, cooperation), credit or debit the INHERITED TRUST the inflow carries. Trust is transmitted across generations and predicts growth; use the trust of descendants-of-immigrants as a time-varying, origin-linked measure (Algan-Cahuc). This is a decade-scale channel invisible to annual fiscal accounting.
generators.retrodiction: Would have surfaced that inherited trust of US immigrant descendants is significantly shaped by their origin country and the timing of their forebears' arrival, and that inherited trust causally predicts cross-country growth — so the composition of inflows has a slow social-capital effect the static ledger omits.
generators.negative_space: Annual net-fiscal and first-gen crime measures cannot see a social-capital stock that shifts over generations with the origin-mix of the population.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Trust/social capital is a host-institutional constant
The composition of inflows does not affect the aggregate trust stock
Social capital has no fiscal or growth value worth pricing
source_rel_paths: DISCONFIRM: Eder 2018 'A replication of Inherited Trust and Growth' (Economics Bulletin); Muller-Torgler-Uslaner 2012 comment — the inherited-trust result has live replication challenges
generators.theory_tested: Origin-linked inherited-trust measures should correlate with 2nd-gen US civic/labor outcomes; the effect must survive the Eder/Muller replication critiques before it is load-bearing.

generators.name: Ethnic capital / slow assimilation brake
generators.prompt: Do not assume the 2nd generation regresses to the host mean in a single step. Skills transmit via BOTH parental inputs AND the average skills of the ethnic group in the parents' generation (ethnic capital); this slows convergence, so origin-skill differentials persist longer than the simple intergenerational-mobility model implies (Borjas). The dynasty-optimism in cluster-T and the ledger-map dynasty row assume faster convergence than ethnic capital permits.
generators.retrodiction: Would have caught that 'the kids catch up' overstates convergence speed: ethnic capital acts as a brake on regression to the mean, keeping low-skill origin groups below the host average for two to three generations rather than one.
generators.negative_space: The positive dynasty/2nd-gen ledger (cluster-T, ledger-map) prices in fast convergence; the ethnic-capital persistence that slows it is the missing debit.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: The 2nd generation converges to the host mean in one generation
Only parental skills, not the ethnic environment, transmit human capital
Convergence speed is origin-invariant
source_rel_paths: Borjas 1995 'Ethnicity, Neighborhoods, and Human-Capital Externalities' AER (NBER w4912) — the neighborhood-externality companion
generators.theory_tested: 2nd-gen US skill/earnings by origin should regress toward the host mean SLOWER than a one-generation model predicts — directly opposed by V08's fast-assimilation evidence; the truth is the decay RATE, measurable in the IPUMS panel.

generators.name: The new economic case for restrictions (institutional-quality externality)
generators.prompt: State the strongest LONG-RUN restrictionist argument in its own terms, then ASSESS it rather than assume it: large inflows from low-productivity-institution origins could transmit those institutions and norms to the host, lowering aggregate TFP — a dynamic externality that, if real and large, swamps any static fiscal gain or loss. The honest move (Clemens-Pritchett pose AND assess this exact case): how strong is transmission, how fast does it decay, and how elastic are host institutions to immigrant share at observed migration scales?
generators.retrodiction: Would have named the single argument that, if true, dominates the entire ledger — and would have applied Clemens-Pritchett's own assessment: the channel is theoretically coherent but the 'diaspora dynamics' and institutional elasticities needed to make restriction welfare-optimal are not supported in the data at current margins. The case survives as a coherent worry, not as a quantified cost.
generators.negative_space: Both S (fiscal) and T (benefit) operate in the proximate/first-gen window; the institutional-transmission externality is the missing long-run term on which the entire restriction case ultimately rests.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Host institutions are immigration-invariant (the optimist's unstated premise)
Host institutions are highly elastic to the immigrant origin-mix (the restrictionist's unstated premise)
Whichever premise is assumed is rarely defended with an elasticity
generators.theory_tested: For restriction to be welfare-optimal on this channel, the elasticity of host institutional quality to immigrant origin-share must exceed a threshold Clemens-Pritchett derive; observed cross-country evidence does not clear it — the lens is a coherent hypothesis pending a credible elasticity estimate.

generators.name: National cognitive/human capital of origin (CONTESTED — stress-test, do not assume)
generators.prompt: The hardest-edged version of the composition argument: measured national average human-capital / cognitive-skill indices predict productivity across countries (Jones-Schneider BACE), so the origin-composition of an inflow could in principle shift host human capital. THIS LENS IS THE STEELMAN TO STRESS-TEST, NOT A PREMISE. Three load-bearing caveats fire automatically: (1) the underlying national-IQ data (Lynn-Vanhanen) is low quality — thin coverage, non-representative samples, fragile age-norming; (2) the cross-country correlation is heavily confounded by institutions, health, schooling, and colonial history; (3) a NATIONAL-AVERAGE correlation licenses NO individual-level or first-generation inference (ecological fallacy). Invoke only to be tested, never to conclude.
generators.retrodiction: Would have both surfaced the Jones-Schneider finding that measured cognitive-skill aggregates robustly predict cross-country growth AND immediately attached the data-quality, confounding, and ecological-inference caveats that make the result unusable for any individual, first-gen, or short-run immigration claim — preventing exactly the over-extension this channel invites.
generators.negative_space: The composition-of-human-capital channel is genuine in the cross-country growth literature but is the most frequently misused; it is absent from the fiscal/crime ledgers and, when invoked in immigration debate, is almost always stretched past what the data support.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: National-average cognitive measures are accurate and cross-country comparable
A national-average correlation licenses individual or first-gen inference
Cognitive skill is exogenous to schooling, health, and institutions rather than co-determined with them
source_rel_paths: Jones-Schneider 2005 'Intelligence, Human Capital, and Economic Growth: BACE' J Econ Growth (DOI 10.2139/SSRN.552481) — saved
Jones 'Hive Mind' (2015) + 'The Culture Transplant' (2023, Stanford UP) — books, [TRAINING-DATA], not in paper corpus
CROSS-REF: ~/Projects/iq-sex-differences for the IQ-measurement and Lynn-data-quality literature — do NOT re-litigate here
generators.theory_tested: Deliberately NOT testable in this fiscal/crime warehouse. Flagged as the lens whose data and whose critiques both live in the sister IQ repo; any use must route through that repo's data-quality verdicts first.

generators.name: Ancestral/cultural/linguistic distance as a diffusion barrier
generators.prompt: Add ancestral, cultural, and linguistic DISTANCE between origin and host as an integration-cost channel. Greater distance slows the diffusion of technology, norms, and institutions between the groups and historically raises inter-group friction (Spolaore-Wacziarg 'Ancestry, Language and Culture'). The implication is a COMPOSITION effect: closer-ancestry inflows integrate more cheaply than distant-ancestry inflows of the same size — a margin the level-based fiscal ledger ignores.
generators.retrodiction: Would have predicted that integration costs and inter-group frictions scale with ancestral/cultural distance, not with immigrant numbers alone — reframing 'how many' debates as partly 'from where', a composition question the headcount-based ledger cannot pose.
generators.negative_space: Fiscal and crime ledgers price the LEVEL of inflows; the distance-dependent integration cost (a composition effect) is unrepresented.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Integration cost is independent of origin-host distance
Only the level, not the composition, of inflows matters
Diffusion of norms/technology between groups is frictionless
source_rel_paths: Spolaore-Wacziarg 'Ancestry, Language and Culture' (NBER w21242; OpenAlex W3125724181) — identified, fetch pending
corpus://doi_10_3386_w18130/paper.pdf (the deep-roots survey covers the distance mechanism)
generators.theory_tested: Integration-cost proxies (language acquisition, intermarriage, residential segregation) should scale with origin-host ancestral/linguistic distance — testable against ACS origin detail, not yet built.

generators.name: Self-rebuttal: assimilation refutes STRONG persistence (constructive null)
generators.prompt: Before reading V01-V07 as transmission-is-destiny, apply the assimilation NULL. US immigrants and their descendants assimilate fast by historical standards (Abramitzky-Boustan), immigrant crime converges DOWN not up (the 1870-2020 incarceration gap; the Texas DPS arrest rates this repo built), and origin persistence DECAYS each generation. Therefore V01-V07 bound a LONG-RUN, AVERAGE, COMPOSITION concern — they do NOT license first-generation, individual-level, or fixed-trait claims, and the US melting-pot record cuts hardest against the strong-transmission version.
generators.retrodiction: Would have stopped every deep-roots lens above from being misread as a first-gen or individual claim: the same micro-data that shows some origin persistence ALSO shows rapid regression toward the host mean and a 150-year immigrant CRIME ADVANTAGE. The honest reading is partial, decaying transmission — not destiny.
generators.negative_space: A steelman without its null is a polemic. This lens is the built-in disconfirmation that keeps cluster-V honest and prevents its long-run, average claims from being laundered into first-gen or individual ones.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Transmission is fixed rather than decaying
Long-run or average persistence implies individual or first-gen outcomes
European low-assimilation evidence transfers to the US melting pot
source_rel_paths: Abramitzky-Boustan-Jacome-Perez 'Law-Abiding Immigrants: The Incarceration Gap 1870-2020' (SSRN 4878020) — saved
in-repo: crime_tx_arrests_by_status, crime_spi_inmates_by_citizenship (the assimilation/advantage evidence this repo built)
generators.theory_tested: 2nd-gen US outcomes by origin should show SIGNIFICANT regression toward the host mean within 1-2 generations (IPUMS panel) — this regression rate is the empirical bound on any persistence found under V02/V04, and the incarceration evidence already shows the crime channel runs PRO-immigrant.

theories_tested.theory: The crime channel of origin-transmission runs PRO-immigrant and does not erode toward a deficit — refuting strong negative cultural transmission for crime (the V08 null, testable in-warehouse now).
theories_tested.prediction: In crime_tx_arrests_by_status, the unauthorized and naturalized rates stay BELOW true native-born across 2012-2018, and the undocumented/native ratio does NOT trend toward 1.0.
theories_tested.duckdb_test: SELECT year, max(CASE WHEN status_class='unauthorized' THEN crime_rate_per_100k END) AS undoc, max(CASE WHEN status_class='native_born' THEN crime_rate_per_100k END) AS native FROM crime_tx_arrests_by_status WHERE crime_category='violent' AND denom_source='CMS_nat' GROUP BY year ORDER BY year
theories_tested.falsifier: Undocumented violent-crime rate rises above true native-born in any year, or the undoc/native ratio trends upward toward 1.0 over 2012-2018.

theories_tested.theory: The long-run institutional-quality externality (V05) requires host institutions to be elastic to immigrant origin-mix at observed scales; Clemens-Pritchett find that elasticity empirically weak — so the restriction case is coherent but not data-supported at current margins.
theories_tested.prediction: No state institutional-quality proxy in this warehouse declines with immigrant origin-share at the magnitude the restriction case needs; the channel cannot be quantified from what is built.
theories_tested.duckdb_test: -- NOT yet buildable: requires a state institutional-quality panel merged on immigrant origin-share (acquisition target). Flagged, not run.
theories_tested.falsifier: A credible elasticity estimate showing host TFP or institutional quality declines with immigrant origin-share at observed migration margins.
